fix: Either use a ref to track mounted state and guard the setState calls, or ensure the timeout is cancelled in a cleanup effect or component unmount handler.

title: Uncleanable timer may cause memory leak warning on unmount
detail: The onCopy callback sets a timer via window.setTimeout at line 112 that calls setCopied(false) after 1.5 seconds. If the component unmounts before the timer fires, React will warn about setting state on an unmounted component. The timer is not aborted on cleanup, making it impossible to prevent the state update.
fix: Use useEffect with a cleanup that aborts the timer, or store the timeout ID in a useRef and clear it on unmount. Example: store timeoutId in useRef, clear it on unmount or before setting new one.

title: Mutable Set used as React state initializer
detail: Line 27 initializes state with `useState<Set<number>>(() => new Set())`. While the initializer function pattern is correct, using mutable Set instances as state is problematic in React. Sets don't implement value equality, so if re-renders occur, the same Set reference from a previous render could cause unexpected behavior. The picked indices should be stored as an immutable structure (e.g., Set is fine for local mutations within setState, but the initialization pattern suggests this may be a source of bugs in parent/sibling updates).
fix: Consider using an immutable structure like `new Set<number>()` initialized inline, but more importantly, verify that Set comparisons work correctly across re-renders. If issues arise with stale Set references, convert to an array-based state: `useState<number[]>(() => [])` and use indexOf/filter for membership tests.

title: Redundant type check in walk function
detail: Line 83 checks `!SKIP_PARENTS.has(node.type)` but this condition is redundant. Line 80 already returns early if `SKIP_PARENTS.has(node.type)` is true, so inside the loop at line 83, `node.type` can never be in SKIP_PARENTS. This check is dead code.
fix: Remove the `!SKIP_PARENTS.has(node.type)` check on line 83 and simply check `if (child.type === "text")` to process text nodes.

title: countLines() counts trailing newline as extra line, inconsistent with linesOf()
detail:
The countLines() function splits text by '
' without removing trailing newlines first, unlike linesOf() at line 107 which does: `text.endsWith("
") ? text.slice(0, -1).split("
") : text.split("
")`. This causes text ending with '
' to be counted 1 line too high. For example, "line1
line2
" would count 3 lines but linesOf() returns 2 lines. This discrepancy affects the added/removed line counts shown in the UI (lines 62-63, 74-75, 83-84, 93-94) which will not match the actual lines in the generated diffs.
fix:
Make countLines() consistent with linesOf(): `return typeof s === "string" && s.length > 0 ? (s.endsWith("
") ? s.slice(0, -1).split("
").length : s.split("
").length) : 0;`

title: Uncleaned setTimeout in onUndoConfirmed creates memory leak
detail: The setTimeout() call on line 68 is never cleaned up. If the component unmounts or the modal closes before the 4-second timeout fires, the callback will still attempt to call setUndoStatus() on an unmounted component. While React will warn about this in development, the proper pattern is to wrap this in a useEffect with a cleanup function that clears the timeout.
fix: Wrap the setTimeout logic in a useEffect with proper cleanup: `useEffect(() => { const timeout = window.setTimeout(() => setUndoStatus(null), 4000); return () => clearTimeout(timeout); }, [undoStatus])` or alternatively store the timeout ID and clear it on unmount.

title: Incorrect sort order for pinned repos — unpinned come first instead of pinned
detail: Line 91: `if (ap !== bp) return bp - ap;` sorts unpinned items before pinned items. Since ap=1 for pinned and ap=0 for unpinned, `bp - ap` gives positive (b before a) when b is unpinned (0) and a is pinned (1). This is backwards from the docstring at lines 82-85 which states 'Pinned first'.
fix: Change line 91 to `return ap - bp;` so pinned items (ap=1) sort before unpinned (ap=0).

title: Missing error handling in commitEdit allows silent failures
detail: The commitEdit function awaits window.codeshell.renameSession(editing, editDraft.trim()) at line 51 without a try-catch block. If the rename fails, the error is silently ignored but the UI state (setEditing(null) and setEditDraft("")) is still cleared at lines 52-53, leaving the UI in an inconsistent state where the user doesn't know the operation failed.
fix: Wrap the renameSession call in a try-catch block and only clear the editing state on success. Show an error message on failure.

title: Unhandled promise rejection in delete button handler
detail: The onClick handler at line 124-127 awaits window.codeshell.deleteSession(s.id) without a try-catch block. If deletion fails, the error is silently ignored but refresh() is still called at line 126, potentially showing stale UI state. Users won't know if deletion succeeded or failed.
fix: Wrap the deleteSession call in a try-catch block. Only call refresh() on success, and display an error message on failure.

title: Unhandled promise rejection in save function
detail: The save function awaits window.codeshell.saveAgent(draft) at line 90 without a try-catch for the success path. If the subsequent load() call at line 91 fails, the error won't propagate to the catch block and the UI will remain stale. Additionally, if saveAgent succeeds but load() fails, the user won't see the error.
fix: Add try-catch around both saveAgent and load calls. Ensure both operations succeed before updating selected state. Display error messages for each operation separately.

title: Missing error handling in remove function
detail: The remove function calls window.codeshell.deleteAgent(a.name) at line 106 without a try-catch block. If deletion fails, the error is silently ignored but UI state is still cleared at lines 107-109 (load() is called, then selected and draft are set to null), leaving the UI inconsistent.
fix: Wrap the deleteAgent call and subsequent load() call in a try-catch block. Only clear UI state on success. Display error messages to the user on failure.

title: HTTP headers formatted as KEY=VALUE instead of Key: Value
detail: Line 414 calls `envOrHeadersToText(initial?.headers)` which formats all key-value pairs as `KEY=VALUE` (line 646). However, HTTP headers should use `Key: Value` format as shown in the textarea placeholder at line 553. This causes headers to be displayed in the wrong format to the user, potentially confusing them or causing issues when they try to re-edit.
fix: Modify `envOrHeadersToText` to accept a format parameter, or create a separate function for headers. Alternatively, in the editor, use different formatting for headers: split on `:` and rejoin with `:` during round-trip.

title: Unhandled promise rejection in load() function
detail: The load() async function calls window.codeshell.getSettings() without wrapping it in a try-catch block (lines 27-33). If the promise rejects, it will trigger an unhandled rejection. Compare to ModelSection.tsx line 228-235 which properly wraps async calls in try-catch.
fix: Wrap the getSettings call in try-catch: try { const s = (await window.codeshell.getSettings(scope, cwd)) ?? {}; ... } catch (e) { console.error(e); }

title: No error handling for updateSettings failure
detail: The choose() function uses try-finally without a catch block. If window.codeshell.updateSettings() throws an error at line 42-48, the error is silently ignored and the user receives no feedback. The setSaving flag is reset to false but the error is never communicated to the user, unlike ModelSection.tsx which maintains and displays an error state.
fix: Add a catch block to handle and display errors: catch (e) { console.error(e); /* display error to user */ } or add error state management like ModelSection has.

title: canFetch condition incorrectly exempts openrouter from API key requirement
detail: Line 476 has condition: (!kindMeta.needsKey || Boolean(effectiveApiKey) || form.kind === "openrouter"). This exempts openrouter from requiring an API key, but KIND_META at line 82-87 shows openrouter has needsKey: true. This logic appears inverted - it should allow fetch without key only for providers where needsKey is false (like ollama at line 137-140), not for openrouter which requires a key.
fix: Remove the `|| form.kind === "openrouter"` clause. The condition should be: `Boolean(effectiveBaseUrl) && (!kindMeta.needsKey || Boolean(effectiveApiKey))`

title: Silent error handling in saveProvider with no user feedback
detail: The catch block in saveProvider (line 159) only resets saving: false but does not communicate the error to the user. If writeBack fails (e.g., due to permission issues or disk problems), the user sees the save button become enabled again but has no indication the save failed. The dirty flag is never reset on error, so users may think they need to resave or may not realize the operation failed.
fix: Add error state tracking to ProviderState (or panel-level) and display error messages to users. Consider setting dirty: true on error so the unsaved change is still visible, or show a toast/error message in the UI.

title: No error handling in clearProvider, risking UI/state desync
detail: clearProvider calls setByProvider(next) immediately (line 169) without awaiting or catching errors from writeBack (line 170). If writeBack fails, the UI will show the provider as cleared while the settings file still contains the old data. On next load, the data will reappear, confusing users.
fix: Wrap writeBack in try-catch and only call setByProvider after a successful write, or revert the state on error. Add error feedback to the user.

title: Cursor can become negative when ArrowDown pressed on empty list
detail: When `filtered.length === 0` and user presses ArrowDown, the cursor is set to `Math.min(0 + 1, 0 - 1)` which evaluates to `-1`. This violates the invariant that cursor should be in range [0, filtered.length - 1]. While the empty state prevents visual issues, it creates an invalid state. The same issue occurs at line 61 for ArrowUp when the cursor is already at 0 on an empty list (though that case is guarded by Math.max(0, ...)).
fix: Guard the cursor update to only apply when filtered.length > 0, or clamp the result: `setCursor((c) => filtered.length > 0 ? Math.min(c + 1, filtered.length - 1) : 0)`

title: Duplicated formatRelative function
detail: The formatRelative function (lines 561-575) is identical to the one in SessionSearchModal.tsx (lines 162-176). Both files define the same time formatting logic independently, violating DRY principle.
fix: Extract formatRelative into a shared utility module (e.g., packages/desktop/src/renderer/utils/formatRelative.ts) and import it in both files.

title: Duplicated formatRelative function
detail: The formatRelative function (lines 162-176) is identical to the one in Sidebar.tsx (lines 561-575). Both files define the same time formatting logic independently, violating DRY principle.
fix: Extract formatRelative into a shared utility module (e.g., packages/desktop/src/renderer/utils/formatRelative.ts) and import it in both files.

title: Write success detection relies on fragile result string heuristic
detail: Lines 74-76 treat a Write/write_file call as successful if the result string doesn't start with 'error' (case-insensitive). This is insufficient: an empty result (line 75 coerces undefined to '') would pass the check and be marked successful even if the write failed. The logic doesn't validate that the result is actually present or meaningful, only that it doesn't start with 'error'. A result like 'Error: timeout' would correctly fail, but 'error' or '' would incorrectly succeed.
fix: Add an explicit check for truthy result before adding the attachment: `const lc = (result ?? "").toLowerCase(); if (lc && !lc.startsWith("error")) push(obj.file_path);` or use a more robust success indicator like checking for 'success' or 'wrote' keywords.

title: Agent tool routing uses overly broad startsWith check
detail: Line 50 routes any tool with a name starting with 'agent' to AgentToolCard: `if (name === "agent" || name === "task" || name.startsWith("agent"))`. This would incorrectly match tools named 'agently', 'agent_foo', etc. if they're not intended agent tools. The first condition `name === "agent"` is redundant with the third condition, and startsWith matching is less precise than explicit name checking.
fix: Replace `name.startsWith("agent")` with explicit matches like `name === "agent" || name === "agent_task" || name === "agent_run"` or document the intended behavior. At minimum, remove the redundant first condition `name === "agent" ||`.

title: Div with onFocus/onBlur handlers is not keyboard-focusable
detail: The div element at lines 98-129 has onFocus and onBlur event handlers (lines 105-106) but divs are not focusable by default in HTML. This means keyboard users cannot trigger the status popover via focus, only mouse users can via onMouseEnter. The div needs tabIndex={0} or similar to be focusable.
fix: Add tabIndex={0} to the div at line 98 to make it keyboard-focusable so focus/blur handlers can work.

title: Unused ref variable confirmRef
detail: Line 74 declares `confirmRef` via `useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null)`, which is attached to the confirm button on line 107, but the ref is never read or used anywhere in the code.
fix: Remove the unused `confirmRef` declaration and remove the `ref={confirmRef}` attribute from the button.

title: Using array index as React key in list
detail: Line 51 uses `key={i}` (array index) for menu items. While this component closes immediately after interaction, using indices as keys is an anti-pattern and can cause issues if items are reordered or dynamically added/removed.
fix: Add a stable unique identifier to `ContextMenuItem` interface and use that for the key instead of index.

title: Redundant type cast in UpdaterBanner
detail: Line 11 casts `s` to `UpdaterStatus` with `(s as UpdaterStatus)`, but the callback parameter `s` is already typed as `UpdaterStatus` from the context. The cast is redundant and adds unnecessary noise.
fix: Remove the cast: `setStatus(s);` instead of `setStatus(s as UpdaterStatus);`

title: Redundant type cast in UpdaterSettingsRow
detail: Line 64 casts `s` to `UpdaterStatus` with `(s as UpdaterStatus)`, but the callback parameter `s` is already typed as `UpdaterStatus`. The cast is redundant.
fix: Remove the cast: `setStatus(s);` instead of `setStatus(s as UpdaterStatus);`

title: Missing error handling in setTrust causes UI to hang permanently on failure
detail: The decide() function at line 27 awaits window.codeshell.setTrust() without a try-catch. If setTrust rejects, the setPending(false) at line 31 never executes, leaving the button disabled forever with no way for the user to retry or recover.
fix: Wrap the setTrust call in try-catch: try { await window.codeshell.setTrust(...) } catch (e) { console.error(e); } finally { setPending(false); }

title: Unhandled promise rejection in getTrust call
detail: The promise returned by window.codeshell.getTrust(repoPath) at line 16 has no .catch() handler. If this promise rejects, it will cause an unhandled promise rejection, even though the cancelled flag check at line 17 prevents state updates. Rejections should be explicitly handled.
fix: Add .catch(err => { if (!cancelled) console.error(err); }) or use try-catch in an async IIFE
